User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in the Build IO application, distinguishing between buyers and developers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

username.description: A unique username chosen by the user.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and login.

role.description: The role of the user, either 'buyer' or 'developer'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user account was updated.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Contains additional personal details for a user, complementing the basic User entity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

userId.description: Reference to the associated User. (Relationship: User 1:1 UserProfile)

bio.description: A short biographical description of the user.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

DeveloperProfile.title: DeveloperProfile
DeveloperProfile.description: Stores specific information for users acting as developers, including their professional details and ratings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DeveloperProfile entity.

userId.description: Reference to the associated User who is a developer. (Relationship: User 1:1 DeveloperProfile)

specialization.description: The primary area of expertise or specialization of the developer.

hourlyRate.description: The developer's hourly rate for services, in USD.

experienceSummary.description: A summary of the developer's professional experience and skills.

averageRating.description: The average rating received by the developer from client reviews.

Technology.title: Technology
Technology.description: Represents a technology or framework used in website development (e.g., React, Node.js).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Technology entity.

WebsiteListing.title: WebsiteListing
WebsiteListing.description: Details about a website listed for sale on the marketplace.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WebsiteListing entity.

title.description: The title or name of the website being sold.

description.description: A detailed description of the website, its features, and benefits.

price.description: The selling price of the website, in USD.

sellerUserId.description: Reference to the User who is selling this website. (Relationship: User 1:N WebsiteListing)

demoUrl.description: URL to a live demonstration or preview of the website.

status.description: The current sales status of the website listing (e.g., 'available', 'sold', 'pending').

technologyIds.description: References to Technologies used in the website. (Relationship: WebsiteListing N:N Technology)

imageUrls.description: URLs to images or screenshots illustrating the website.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the website listing was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the website listing was updated.

PortfolioItem.title: PortfolioItem
PortfolioItem.description: Represents a completed project or work sample displayed in a developer's portfolio.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PortfolioItem entity.

developerProfileId.description: Reference to the DeveloperProfile to whom this portfolio item belongs. (Relationship: DeveloperProfile 1:N PortfolioItem)

title.description: The title of the portfolio item or project.

description.description: A description of the project, including technologies used and the developer's role.

projectUrl.description: URL to the live project or a detailed case study.

imageUrls.description: URLs to images or screenshots showcasing the portfolio item.

completionDate.description: The date when the project was completed.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Tracks a transaction, either for a website purchase or a service engagement.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

buyerUserId.description: Reference to the User who initiated the purchase or service request. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

sellerUserId.description: Reference to the User (seller of website or developer) involved in the transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

websiteListingId.description: Optional reference to the WebsiteListing purchased if the order is for a website. (Relationship: WebsiteListing 1:1 Order)

serviceEngagementId.description: Optional reference to the ServiceEngagement if the order is for a developer service. (Relationship: ServiceEngagement 1:1 Order)

orderDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the order was placed.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'completed', 'cancelled', 'refunded').

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, in USD.

ServiceEngagement.title: ServiceEngagement
ServiceEngagement.description: Details a specific service request or contract between a client and a developer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ServiceEngagement entity.

developerProfileId.description: Reference to the DeveloperProfile providing the service. (Relationship: DeveloperProfile 1:N ServiceEngagement)

clientId.description: Reference to the User who requested the service. (Relationship: User 1:N ServiceEngagement)

description.description: A detailed description of the service requested or agreed upon.

startDate.description: The planned start date for the service engagement.

endDate.description: The planned end date for the service engagement.

status.description: The current status of the service engagement (e.g., 'requested', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'cancelled', 'rejected').

agreedPrice.description: The agreed-upon price for the service engagement, in USD.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order associated with this service engagement. (Relationship: Order 1:1 ServiceEngagement)

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Captures feedback and a rating provided by a user for a developer's services.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

developerProfileId.description: Reference to the DeveloperProfile being reviewed. (Relationship: DeveloperProfile 1:N Review)

reviewerUserId.description: Reference to the User who submitted the review. (Relationship: User 1:N Review)

rating.description: The rating given by the reviewer, typically on a scale (e.g., 1 to 5).

comment.description: Textual feedback provided by the reviewer.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the review was submitted.

definition.description: Root collection for all user-related data, uniquely identified by their authentication UID. **Authorization is path-based: only the authenticated user can read/write their own document.**

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Contains additional personal details for a user. Stored as a subcollection under the user's path, inheriting strong ownership. **Authorization is path-based: only the authenticated user can read/write their own profile.**

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

definition.description: Stores professional information for users with the 'developer' role. This profile is publicly readable, but **only the authenticated developer (owner) can write/update it.** The document will contain `userId` matching the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the developer user.

definition.description: Represents a completed project in a developer's portfolio. Publicly readable for showcasing, but **only the owning developer can create, update, or delete their own portfolio items.** The document **includes a denormalized 'developerUserId'** for authorization independence, enabling rules without `get()` calls to the parent `developerProfile`.

params.name: developerUserId
params.description: The unique ID of the developer user who owns this portfolio item.

params.name: portfolioItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the portfolio item.

definition.description: Stores client reviews for a developer. Publicly readable to display developer ratings. **Only the `reviewerUserId` can create, update, or delete their review.** The document **includes denormalized 'developerUserId' and 'reviewerUserId'** for authorization independence.

params.name: developerUserId
params.description: The unique ID of the developer being reviewed.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique ID of the review.

definition.description: Global collection for predefined technologies used in website listings or developer profiles. **Publicly readable by all users, write access restricted to administrators.**

params.name: technologyId
params.description: The unique ID of the technology.

definition.description: Lists websites available for sale on the marketplace. **Publicly readable for browsing, but only the `sellerUserId` (owner) can create, update, or delete their listings.** The `sellerUserId` field is **denormalized within each document for authorization independence and QAPs** (Query-as-Prove-ability).

params.name: websiteListingId
params.description: The unique ID of the website listing.

definition.description: Records transactions between buyers and sellers/developers. **Access is restricted to either the `buyerUserId` or the `sellerUserId` involved in the transaction.** Both `buyerUserId` and `sellerUserId` are **denormalized within each document for authorization independence and QAPs**, allowing direct rule evaluation.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique ID of the order.

definition.description: Details a service request or contract between a client and a developer. **Access is restricted to either the `clientId` or the `developerUserId` involved.** The document **includes denormalized 'clientId' and 'developerUserId'** for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: serviceEngagementId
params.description: The unique ID of the service engagement.

definition.description: A collection used for **Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC)** to identify administrators. The mere **existence** of a document at `/adminRoles/{uid}` grants admin privileges to that `uid`. **This ensures authorization independence for global roles.** The schema refers to 'User' as it's primarily an identifier check.

params.name: uid
params.description: The unique ID of a user with administrator privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for Build IO is designed with a strong emphasis on **Authorization Independence**, **Structural Segregation**, and **Query-as-Prove-ability (QAPs)**, adhering to the specified core design principles and strategy mandates. The goal is to simplify security rules, make authorization intent explicit, and ensure scalability and debuggability.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
Crucially, authorization context is denormalized across various collections to eliminate the need for `get()` calls in security rules. For instance:
*   In `/websiteListings/{websiteListingId}`, the `sellerUserId` is embedded directly in the document. This allows a rule like `allow write: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.sellerUserId;` without having to look up a parent user document.
*   Similarly, for `/orders/{orderId}` and `/serviceEngagements/{serviceEngagementId}`, both `buyerUserId` and `sellerUserId`/`clientId` and `developerUserId` are present in the document. This enables robust access control for multi-party transactions without complex lookups.
*   For subcollections like `/users/{developerUserId}/developerProfile/portfolioItems/{portfolioItemId}` and `/users/{developerUserId}/developerProfile/reviews/{reviewId}`, the `developerUserId` and `reviewerUserId` (where applicable) are explicitly included in the subcollection document. This ensures that authorization can be evaluated solely based on the document itself and the authenticated user's ID, rather than relying on the parent path's ID.

**Structural Segregation for Homogeneous Security Posture & QAPs:**
Collections are structured to group documents with similar security requirements, greatly simplifying security rules and supporting QAPs:
*   **Private User Data:** `/users/{userId}` and its immediate subcollections (`/userProfile`, `/developerProfile`) are primarily owned by the `request.auth.uid`, ensuring that sensitive user data is secured by path-based authorization.
*   **Public/Marketplace Data with Owner Management:** `/websiteListings/{websiteListingId}` is a top-level collection. It's publicly readable, enabling direct queries for the marketplace (QAPs for `list` operations), while write/update access is strictly limited to the `sellerUserId` embedded within each listing document. This allows users to query the entire marketplace without pre-filtering on the client side, and rules ensure only authorized writes.
*   **Global Read-Only Data:** `/technologies/{technologyId}` is a separate collection for static, global lookup data, easily allowing read-all access with admin-only write permissions.
*   **Multi-Party Transaction Data:** `/orders/{orderId}` and `/serviceEngagements/{serviceEngagementId}` are top-level collections, explicitly containing all relevant `userId`s (`buyerUserId`, `sellerUserId`, `clientId`, `developerUserId`) for direct access control. This supports QAPs by allowing users to query their orders/engagements efficiently using `where('buyerUserId', '==', request.auth.uid)` or `where('sellerUserId', '==', request.auth.uid)` without breaking security.
*   **DBAC for Global Roles:** A dedicated `/adminRoles/{uid}` collection is used for Database-Backed Access Control. The **existence** of a document at this path determines if a user is an admin, making admin checks simple and highly performant (QAPs on `get(/adminRoles/request.auth.uid)`).

This structure ensures that each document inherently carries the necessary authorization context, allowing for atomic operations and enabling secure `list` queries without `get()` calls in rules, thereby upholding the core design principles.
